title: The DTO pattern
description: Why every Shift entity has two DTOs (ViewAndUpsert and List), how they differ, and when each one is used.

title: The mapping abstraction
description: How the repository translates between entities and DTOs, what IShiftEntityMapper is, and how to choose between AutoMapper, Manual, Mapperly, and Mapster mapping strategies.

title: Repositories and the request lifecycle
description: How a request flows from the controller through the repository to the database and back, where business logic lives, and how audit fields and soft deletes happen automatically along the way.

title: The ShiftEntity model
description: What you get for free when you inherit from the ShiftEntity base class — IDs, audit fields, soft delete, the ReloadAfterSave hook, and temporal history.

title: Installation
description: Install prerequisites, the dotnet new template, and scaffold your first Shift Framework project.

title: Project tour
description: What each project in a generated Shift Framework solution does, and how the pieces fit together.

title: Run the generated sample
description: Bring the scaffolded app up end-to-end with a database, migrations, and a running API.

title: Template parameters
description: Every parameter the dotnet new shift template accepts, what it controls, and when to change it.

title: What is Shift Framework?
description: An opinionated full-stack .NET framework for building line-of-business applications.

title: Your first entity
description: Use the shiftentity item template to scaffold a new entity end-to-end across all four projects.
